items.title: marrow-core
items.text:
最小化自演化 agent 调度器，严格隔离核心与演化层。

marrow-core 是一个纯粹的 service/runtime 内核，负责 agent 调度、服务生命周期和运行时自检。它不携带默认配置文件、角色树或 casting 流程——这些均由外部 profile 仓库（如 marrow-bot）提供。

items.title: 核心职责
items.text: 职责 说明 服务生命周期 run / run-once 调度心跳循环 调度 按配置触发各 agent 的心跳 Sync 与自检 sync-once、validate、doctor IPC 状态控制 通过 Unix socket 查询和控制运行时 即时触发 wake 唤醒 agent（可附带一次性 prompt） 服务安装 渲染 launchd / systemd 服务描述文件

items.title: 不包含的内容
items.text:
• 任务队列与工作项契约 → 见 marrow-task
  • 人工工作流状态机 → 见 loom
  • 角色树与 prompt 策略 → 见外部 profile（marrow-bot）

items.title: 快速开始
items.text:
# 验证配置
uvx marrow-core validate --config /path/to/runtime-config.toml

# 以服务方式运行
uvx marrow-core run --config /path/to/runtime-config.toml

# 安装为系统服务
uvx marrow-core install-service --config /path/to/runtime-config.toml --platform auto --output-dir ./service-out

更多内容请见 快速入门 和 CLI 参考。

items.title: CLI 参考
items.text: marrow-core 暴露单一 marrow 命令，所有子命令均围绕 service/runtime 定位设计。

items.title: 命令概览
items.text: 命令 分组 说明 run 调度 启动持续调度循环（supervisor 或单用户心跳） run-once 调度 每个 agent 执行一次心跳后退出 dry-run 调度 构建 prompt 但不运行 agent sync-once 同步 执行一次有界 sync 尝试 validate 检查 验证配置并打印摘要 doctor 检查 检查工作目录与命令健康状态 setup 初始化 初始化运行时目录和工作目录 scaffold 初始化 创建起始工作目录和配置 install-service 服务 渲染服务描述文件（launchd/systemd） status 运行时 通过 IPC 查询运行时状态 wake 运行时 唤醒 agent，可附带一次性 prompt

items.title: marrow run
items.text:
marrow run [--config PATH]

启动根 supervisor 或单用户心跳循环。会持续运行直到手动终止。

items.title: marrow run-once
items.text:
marrow run-once [--config PATH]

对每个已配置的 agent 各执行一次心跳后退出。适合调试和 CI。

items.title: marrow dry-run
items.text:
marrow dry-run [--config PATH]

构建每个 agent 的 prompt 但不实际执行，打印 prompt 摘要。

items.title: marrow sync-once
items.text:
marrow sync-once [--config PATH]

执行一次有界的 sync 操作（如拉取外部 context 或状态同步）。

items.title: marrow validate
items.text:
marrow validate [--config PATH]

验证 runtime config 文件格式和内容，打印配置摘要。不启动任何服务。

items.title: marrow doctor
items.text:
marrow doctor [--config PATH]

检查工作目录结构、所需命令可用性和运行时健康状态。

items.title: marrow setup
items.text:
marrow setup [--config PATH]

初始化运行时目录和 agent 工作目录。通常在首次部署时运行。

items.title: marrow scaffold
items.text:
marrow scaffold [--output-dir PATH]

在指定目录创建起始工作目录和示例配置，方便新部署快速上手。

items.title: marrow install-service
items.text:
marrow install-service [--config PATH] [--platform auto|launchd|systemd] [--output-dir PATH]

渲染服务描述文件到 --output-dir，不自动安装。支持 macOS launchd 和 Linux systemd。

items.title: marrow status
items.text:
marrow status [--config PATH]

通过 Unix socket IPC 查询当前运行时状态（需要服务正在运行）。

items.title: marrow wake
items.text:
marrow wake [--agent AGENT_ID] [--prompt TEXT] [--config PATH]

唤醒指定 agent，可附带一次性 prompt 覆盖默认心跳行为。

items.title: 推荐：通过 uvx 运行（无需本地安装）
items.text:
uvx marrow-core validate --config /path/to/runtime-config.toml
uvx marrow-core run --config /path/to/runtime-config.toml

items.title: 本地开发安装
items.text:
git clone https://github.com/zrr1999/marrow-core
cd marrow-core
uv sync --all-groups

items.title: 使用前提
items.text: marrow-core 需要一个外部 runtime config（通常由外部 profile 仓库如 marrow-bot 提供）。不要在 marrow-core 仓库内放置 profile 资产。

items.title: 1. 验证配置
items.text: uvx marrow-core validate --config /path/to/runtime-config.toml

items.title: 2. 健康检查
items.text: uvx marrow-core doctor --config /path/to/runtime-config.toml

items.title: 3. 脚手架初始化工作目录
items.text: uvx marrow-core scaffold --output-dir ./my-workspace

items.title: 4. 安装为系统服务
items.text:
# 渲染服务描述文件（不自动安装）
uvx marrow-core install-service \
  --config /path/to/runtime-config.toml \
  --platform auto \
  --output-dir ./service-out

items.title: 5. 运行调度器
items.text:
# 持续运行
uvx marrow-core run --config /path/to/runtime-config.toml

# 仅执行一轮（调试用）
uvx marrow-core run-once --config /path/to/runtime-config.toml

# 构建 prompt 但不执行（dry run）
uvx marrow-core dry-run --config /path/to/runtime-config.toml

items.title: 文档本地预览
items.text:
本项目使用 zensical 构建文档。

# 安装文档依赖
uv sync --group dev

# 本地预览（http://localhost:8000）
just docs

# 构建静态站点
just docs-build

items.title: 架构概览
items.text: marrow-core 是一个最小化 service/runtime 内核，遵循严格的核心/演化层隔离原则。

items.title: 设计原则
items.text:
1. 核心不携带 profile — 角色树、prompt 策略、model 映射均由外部 profile 仓库提供
  2. 单一 CLI 入口 — 所有用户接口通过 marrow 命令统一暴露
  3. service-only 定位 — 核心只负责运行时调度，不涉及任务队列或工作项契约

items.title: 关键模块
items.text: 模块 职责 contracts.py 运行时清单与工作目录拓扑规则 heartbeat.py 按配置的顶层 agent 执行调度编排 runtime.py socket、队列、二进制路径解析 ipc.py 通过 Unix socket 实现本地控制平面 services.py launchd/systemd 服务描述渲染 health.py doctor 和自检健康检查 scaffold.py 工作目录脚手架与起始配置生成 prompting.py context 执行与 prompt 组装

items.title: Prompt 层模型
items.text:
外部 profile rules/        ← 稳定的全局策略
外部 profile roles/        ← 规范角色定义与委托边界
外部 profile context.d/    ← 动态队列、状态、环境事实
skills/                    ← prompt 层契约之外的可复用过程

仓库根目录的 agents/ 已退役，不要在其中添加 prompt 内容。

items.title: 原则
items.text: marrow-core 不携带任何默认 profile 资产。所有 profile 内容由外部仓库提供。

items.title: Profile 资产归属
items.text: 资产 归属位置 说明 roles/ 外部 profile 仓库 规范角色定义与委托边界 roles.toml 外部 profile 仓库 model 层级映射与元数据 rules/ 外部 profile 仓库 稳定的全局策略 context.d/ 外部 profile 仓库 动态队列、状态、环境事实 skills/ 外部 profile 仓库 prompt 层契约之外的可复用过程

items.title: 已退役的资产
items.text:
以下资产已从 marrow-core 迁移至外部仓库，不要在 marrow-core 中重新添加：

  • lib.sh / setup.sh
  • context.d/
  • prompts/
  • marrow.toml
  • roles.toml
  • 仓库根目录的 agents/

items.title: 部署模式
items.text:
# 外部 profile 通过 role-forge 渲染后传给 marrow-core
uvx role-forge cast --profile /path/to/marrow-bot

# 将渲染结果的路径传给 marrow-core
uvx marrow-core run --config /path/to/runtime-config.toml

参考：快速入门 | Service 边界

items.title: marrow-core 拥有
items.text:
• 服务生命周期：启动、停止、重启调度循环
  • 调度：按配置触发各 agent 的心跳
  • Sync 与自检：一次性 sync、validate、doctor
  • IPC 控制平面：通过 Unix socket 查询和控制运行时状态
  • 即时触发：wake 命令唤醒 agent（可附带一次性 prompt）
  • 服务安装：渲染 launchd / systemd 服务描述文件
  • 脚手架：初始化工作目录和起始配置

items.title: marrow-core 不拥有
items.text: 职责 归属 任务队列与工作项契约 marrow-task / loom 人工工作流状态机 loom 外部工作流编排策略 外部 profile 角色树与 prompt 策略 外部 profile（如 marrow-bot） model 映射与 casting 流程 外部 profile + role-forge

items.title: 运行时边界文件
items.text:
• marrow_core/contracts.py — 角色清单与工作目录拓扑
  • marrow_core/heartbeat.py — 调度编排入口
  • marrow_core/ipc.py — Unix socket 控制平面

items.title: Legacy asset migration status
items.text:
The following profile-coupled assets have been removed from marrow-core and should live in external repos:

  • lib.sh
  • setup.sh
  • context.d/
  • prompts/
  • marrow.toml
  • roles.toml

items.title: stays in core
items.text:
• runtime scheduler
  • CLI
  • IPC
  • health checks
  • service rendering
  • work-item contract
  • plugin/background-service host contract

items.title: moves out of core
items.text:
• profile prompts and rules
  • context providers
  • role definitions and casting config
  • deployment-specific sample config
  • opinionated setup wrappers

items.title: Current migration path
items.text:
• marrow-core no longer needs in-repo prompt rules as the default prompt source.
  • install-service now works from the provided runtime config path instead of assuming marrow-core/marrow.toml.
  • new recommended flow is external-profile-first.

Compatibility is now expected to come from external runtime/profile repos rather than in-repo defaults.

items.title: Direction
items.text:
marrow-core becomes the stable service/runtime kernel.

It owns: - service lifecycle - scheduling - sync and self-check - IPC status/control - immediate trigger with optional one-shot prompt - service installation/rendering

It does not own: - task queueing - work-item contracts - human workflow state machines - external workflow orchestration policy

Those move to external repos.

items.title: New Product Shape
items.text:
One public CLI sits on top of the runtime core.

  • root commands are the normal user-facing interface
  • service remains only as an internal/logical grouping for runtime mechanics
  • no separate agent command surface

Current command shape:

  • marrow run
  • marrow run-once
  • marrow dry-run
  • marrow sync-once
  • marrow setup
  • marrow validate
  • marrow doctor
  • marrow scaffold
  • marrow install-service
  • marrow status
  • marrow wake --reason ... --prompt ...

items.title: Runtime Boundary
items.text:
Keep these modules as the active core boundary:

  • marrow_core/config.py
  • marrow_core/runtime.py
  • marrow_core/heartbeat.py
  • marrow_core/ipc.py
  • marrow_core/worker.py
  • marrow_core/services.py
  • marrow_core/health.py
  • marrow_core/sync.py
  • marrow_core/triggers.py

Keep CLI assembly under:

  • marrow_core/cli/service.py
  • marrow_core/cli/ops.py
  • marrow_core/cli/__main__.py

items.title: Trigger Model
items.text:
Replace task submission with trigger-only control.

Trigger payload:

{"agent":"orchestrator","reason":"manual wake","prompt":"Focus on startup recovery."}

Semantics:

  • wake the selected agent immediately
  • attach prompt only to the next run
  • do not persist into context.d
  • consume after one run

This is represented in-memory for single-user mode and via worker request files in supervisor mode.

items.title: Public root commands
items.text:
• run
  • run-once
  • dry-run
  • sync-once
  • setup
  • validate
  • doctor
  • scaffold
  • install-service
  • status
  • wake --reason ... --prompt ...

Rules:

  • one normal command surface
  • human-readable by default
  • stable enough for scripts, but no separate machine persona

items.title: Internal service commands
items.text:
Still exist for implementation structure:

  • service worker-run
  • service workspace-sync

These are not the main public interface.

items.title: Config Evolution
items.text:
Current config still uses [[agents]] as the scheduling inventory. That is acceptable for the transition.

Next cleanup step:

  • rename agent runtime fields to service-oriented names
  • reduce config to service-owned concerns only
  • keep external workflow/task repos responsible for their own state and schemas

Potential future shape:

[service]
mode = "supervisor"
runtime_root = "/var/lib/marrow"

[control]
socket_path = "/var/lib/marrow/state/marrow.sock"

[[service.agents]]
name = "orchestrator"
command = "opencode run --agent orchestrator"
workspace = "/Users/marrow"
interval_seconds = 10800
timeout_seconds = 7200

items.title: Removed from active core
items.text:
• filesystem task queue
  • IPC /tasks
  • marrow task add
  • marrow task list
  • active work_items model in marrow_core/
  • separate agent CLI surface

items.title: Backed up only
items.text:
• legacy/task_queue.py
  • legacy/work_items.py

Nothing in active runtime code should import from legacy/.

items.title: Pass 1: stabilize the unified CLI
items.text:
• finish test updates for the unified root command tree
  • update README/examples/docs to remove task ownership and agent wording
  • keep only hidden internal runtime commands where needed

items.title: Pass 2: simplify service core
items.text:
• move more runtime orchestration out of CLI modules into pure service modules
  • make CLI wrappers thinner
  • reduce overlap between service.py and ops.py

items.title: Pass 3: harden control plane
items.text:
• define explicit JSON schema for /status and /wake
  • add structured trigger request persistence rules for supervisor mode
  • decide whether filtered status views are needed

items.title: Pass 4: shrink internal command leakage
items.text:
• keep worker-run and workspace-sync internal-only
  • keep service manager output and docs aligned with public root commands

items.title: Design Principles
items.text:
• service core stays small and boring
  • one public CLI is easier to remember and maintain
  • tasks belong outside the runtime kernel
  • one-shot prompts are triggers, not queue items

items.title: Goal
items.text: Replace the separate sync service and timer with a single cross-platform runtime model that keeps update behavior simple, bounded, and recoverable.

items.title: Constraints
items.text:
• Prefer one cross-platform implementation over platform-specific watchers or schedulers.
  • Keep the service model minimal: one long-running service, one sync path, one place to observe failures.
  • Avoid hot-reload complexity when a full process restart is simpler and safer.
  • Prevent sync failures from taking down the main heartbeat loop.

items.title: Current State
items.text:
Today the project has two execution paths:

  • marrow run provides the main heartbeat loop.
  • sync.sh provides repository update, dependency refresh, workspace sync, and service reinstall.

This split creates a few problems:

  • Linux sync freshness depends on marrow-heart-sync.timer, currently only once per day.
  • macOS and Linux both carry a second service path just for sync.
  • The core update path lives in shell scripts, while the runtime logic lives in Python CLI code.
  • Sync and heartbeat failures are hard to reason about together because they happen in different processes with different entrypoints.

items.title: Proposed Model
items.text:
Use one persistent service on both platforms:

  • macOS: com.marrow.heart.plist
  • Linux: marrow-heart.service

Move sync orchestration into the CLI with two layers:

  1. marrow run Runs the heartbeat loop and a lightweight periodic sync supervisor.
  2. marrow sync-once Performs exactly one sync attempt and returns a structured outcome.

The supervisor remains intentionally small. It only decides when to invoke sync-once, records basic state, and reacts to the result. It does not directly perform git, uv, or file mutation steps inside the main loop.

items.title: Why This Is The Minimal Cross-Platform Design
items.text:
This approach keeps the implementation restrained:

  • No platform-specific file watching such as inotify or FSEvents
  • No separate sync service or timer lifecycle
  • No complex in-process hot-swapping of code or dependencies
  • No persistent worker pool or supervision tree beyond the main service manager

It uses the same mechanism on macOS and Linux: a normal long-running process with internal periodic polling.

items.title: Sync Execution Boundary
items.text:
marrow run should invoke marrow sync-once in a subprocess, not by directly calling the sync logic inline.

Reasons:

  • git and uv are higher-risk operations than normal heartbeat scheduling
  • subprocess boundaries limit crash radius
  • return codes provide a simple contract
  • the main loop can keep running if sync fails transiently

This keeps policy in the runtime and dangerous mutation in a smaller execution unit.

items.title: Result Contract
items.text:
marrow sync-once should return one of a very small number of outcomes:

  • noop: nothing changed
  • reloaded: only lightweight runtime data changed, and the current process can safely reload
  • restart_required: the current process should exit so the service manager restarts it
  • failed: sync attempt did not complete successfully

This can be represented by exit codes plus a small JSON state file under runtime/state/.

Suggested state fields:

{
  "last_attempt_at": "2026-03-08T00:00:00Z",
  "last_success_at": "2026-03-08T00:00:00Z",
  "result": "noop",
  "reason": "remote unchanged"
}

The state file is diagnostic only. The control flow should stay small enough that exit codes remain the primary contract.

items.title: What sync-once Does
items.text:
marrow sync-once should absorb the useful parts of sync.sh:

  1. Check whether the core repository has a remote update available.
  2. Abort if the worktree is dirty.
  3. Fast-forward to the new revision when safe.
  4. Refresh dependencies when needed.
  5. Refresh workspace directories and role symlinks.
  6. Re-render service files if templates changed.
  7. Decide whether the live process can reload or should restart.

The command should stay conservative. If there is uncertainty about process safety after a change, prefer restart_required over a deeper hot-reload path.

items.title: Reload Rules
items.text:
Safe to reload in-process:

  • role markdown changes
  • workspace directory creation
  • symlink refresh
  • config changes that only affect future scheduling state and can be reapplied cleanly

Require full restart:

  • CLI code changes
  • Python dependency changes
  • service template changes
  • changes that alter process bootstrap assumptions
  • any update whose safety cannot be cheaply proven

This rule set favors clarity over maximum liveness.

items.title: Crash Containment
items.text:
The system needs only a few protections:

  • subprocess isolation for sync execution
  • a lock file so only one sync attempt runs at a time
  • exponential backoff after failures
  • dirty-worktree refusal to avoid destructive updates

These protections are intentionally enough, but not more than enough.

items.title: Service Rendering Changes
items.text:
install-service should render only the primary service files:

  • com.marrow.heart.plist
  • marrow-heart.service

The repo should stop generating:

  • com.marrow.heart.sync.plist
  • marrow-heart-sync.service
  • marrow-heart-sync.timer

Related shell helpers should also be simplified so setup installs one service definition instead of maintaining a second sync lifecycle.

items.title: Testing Strategy
items.text:
The main regression coverage should focus on:

  • service rendering no longer includes sync timer/service artifacts
  • install-service reports the reduced file set
  • sync-once outcome classification
  • run behavior when sync reports noop, reloaded, restart_required, or failure
  • failure backoff and lock behavior

The tests should prefer small unit and black-box CLI coverage over integration-heavy service tests.

items.title: Non-Goals
items.text:
This design does not attempt to provide:

  • sub-second local file watching
  • zero-downtime code reloads after dependency or runtime changes
  • platform-specific optimizations
  • a separate supervisor daemon

Those can be added later only if the simple polling model proves insufficient.

items.title: CLI-Integrated Sync Implementation Plan
items.text:
For Claude: REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

Goal: Replace the separate sync service/timer path with a single cross-platform CLI-managed sync loop that keeps heartbeat execution isolated from risky update work.

Architecture: marrow run stays the only long-running service entrypoint and gains a lightweight periodic sync supervisor. The supervisor launches marrow sync-once as a subprocess, consumes a small result contract, reloads safe changes in-process, and exits for unsafe changes so the existing service manager can restart cleanly.

Tech Stack: Python 3.12, Typer CLI, asyncio, subprocess execution, JSON state files, launchd/systemd service templates, pytest

items.title: Task 1: Define the reduced service model
items.text:
Files: - Modify: marrow_core/services.py - Test: tests/test_services.py - Test: tests/test_cli.py - Test: tests/test_agent_layout.py

Step 1: Write the failing tests

Update the service rendering assertions so they expect only:

  • com.marrow.heart.plist on macOS
  • marrow-heart.service on Linux

Also update the CLI install-service test and the repo layout test so they stop expecting sync timer/service artifacts.

Step 2: Run tests to verify they fail

Run:

pytest tests/test_services.py tests/test_cli.py tests/test_agent_layout.py -q

Expected:

  • failures referencing removed sync service/timer/plist expectations

Step 3: Write the minimal implementation

Change render_service_files() so it renders only the primary service definitions, and update any related output-count assumptions.

Step 4: Run tests to verify they pass

Run:

pytest tests/test_services.py tests/test_cli.py tests/test_agent_layout.py -q

Expected:

  • all targeted tests pass

Step 5: Commit

git add marrow_core/services.py tests/test_services.py tests/test_cli.py tests/test_agent_layout.py
git commit -m "refactor: collapse service model to one runtime service"

items.title: Task 2: Introduce a single sync result contract
items.text:
Files: - Create: marrow_core/sync.py - Modify: marrow_core/cli.py - Test: tests/test_cli.py - Test: tests/test_sync.py

Step 1: Write the failing tests

Add tests for a minimal sync API, covering:

  • sync-once returns noop when no update is available
  • sync-once returns restart_required when code or dependency changes require a restart
  • sync-once refuses to proceed on a dirty worktree

Prefer testing a pure function that maps sync observations to a result enum or dataclass before wiring the CLI command.

Step 2: Run tests to verify they fail

Run:

pytest tests/test_sync.py tests/test_cli.py -q

Expected:

  • import errors or missing command failures for the new sync surface

Step 3: Write the minimal implementation

Create a small marrow_core/sync.py module containing:

  • a SyncResult enum or dataclass
  • a function that evaluates a single sync attempt
  • optional helpers for serializing a tiny JSON status payload

Add a sync-once Typer command in marrow_core/cli.py that delegates to this module and exits with a small set of well-defined codes.

Step 4: Run tests to verify they pass

Run:

pytest tests/test_sync.py tests/test_cli.py -q

Expected:

  • the new sync result tests pass
  • the new CLI command is available and behaves as expected

Step 5: Commit

git add marrow_core/sync.py marrow_core/cli.py tests/test_sync.py tests/test_cli.py
git commit -m "feat: add single-shot sync command contract"

items.title: Task 3: Move shell sync behavior into Python sync helpers
items.text:
Files: - Modify: marrow_core/sync.py - Modify: marrow_core/workspace.py - Test: tests/test_sync.py - Test: tests/test_workspace.py

Step 1: Write the failing tests

Add tests for the absorbed sync behavior:

  • dirty worktree blocks update
  • role symlinks refresh after a successful sync
  • workspace directories are ensured during sync

Mock subprocess calls rather than hitting real git or uv.

Step 2: Run tests to verify they fail

Run:

pytest tests/test_sync.py tests/test_workspace.py -q

Expected:

  • failures showing the new sync workflow is not yet performing the expected steps

Step 3: Write the minimal implementation

Move the useful sync.sh behavior into Python helpers, keeping execution conservative:

  • fetch and compare remote revision
  • reject dirty worktrees
  • fast-forward safely
  • refresh the virtual environment when needed
  • ensure workspace directories
  • refresh role symlinks

Do not attempt deep hot-reload logic here. The output should still collapse into noop, reloaded, restart_required, or failure.

Step 4: Run tests to verify they pass

Run:

pytest tests/test_sync.py tests/test_workspace.py -q

Expected:

  • the new sync workflow tests pass with mocked external commands

Step 5: Commit

git add marrow_core/sync.py marrow_core/workspace.py tests/test_sync.py tests/test_workspace.py
git commit -m "refactor: move sync workflow into Python runtime"

items.title: Task 4: Add a lightweight supervisor inside run
items.text:
Files: - Modify: marrow_core/cli.py - Modify: marrow.toml - Test: tests/test_cli.py

Step 1: Write the failing tests

Add CLI/runtime tests that verify:

  • run schedules the heartbeat loop and the sync supervisor together
  • supervisor ignores noop
  • supervisor triggers an in-process reload on reloaded
  • supervisor exits the process cleanly on restart_required
  • supervisor backs off after failures

Use monkeypatching to fake subprocess execution and timers.

Step 2: Run tests to verify they fail

Run:

pytest tests/test_cli.py -q

Expected:

  • failures for missing supervisor behavior

Step 3: Write the minimal implementation

Extend the runtime with:

  • a small configurable poll interval
  • a lock or in-memory guard preventing overlapping sync attempts
  • subprocess invocation of marrow sync-once
  • conservative handling of result codes

Keep the supervisor logic small. It should orchestrate, not own sync behavior.

Step 4: Run tests to verify they pass

Run:

pytest tests/test_cli.py -q

Expected:

  • the run-loop sync behavior is covered and passing

Step 5: Commit

git add marrow_core/cli.py marrow.toml tests/test_cli.py
git commit -m "feat: add periodic sync supervision to run loop"

items.title: Task 5: Simplify setup and remove the secondary service path
items.text:
Files: - Modify: lib.sh - Modify: setup.sh - Delete: sync.sh - Delete: com.marrow.heart.sync.plist - Delete: marrow-heart-sync.service - Delete: marrow-heart-sync.timer - Test: tests/test_agent_layout.py

Step 1: Write the failing tests

Update layout and behavior tests so they reflect the simplified service model and no longer require the removed files.

Step 2: Run tests to verify they fail

Run:

pytest tests/test_agent_layout.py -q

Expected:

  • failures referencing files or setup behavior tied to the removed sync service path

Step 3: Write the minimal implementation

Simplify shell helpers so setup:

  • renders only the main service
  • installs only the main service
  • shows only the main service status

Delete the obsolete sync shell entrypoint and committed service templates.

Step 4: Run tests to verify they pass

Run:

pytest tests/test_agent_layout.py -q

Expected:

  • layout and setup assumptions now match the single-service design

Step 5: Commit

git add lib.sh setup.sh tests/test_agent_layout.py
git rm sync.sh com.marrow.heart.sync.plist marrow-heart-sync.service marrow-heart-sync.timer
git commit -m "refactor: remove dedicated sync service path"

items.title: Task 6: Document the new operational model
items.text:
Files: - Modify: README.md - Modify: AGENTS.md - Test: tests/test_agent_layout.py

Step 1: Write the failing tests

Add or update assertions for:

  • the new sync-once CLI command
  • the reduced service model
  • removal of sync.sh from documented architecture

Step 2: Run tests to verify they fail

Run:

pytest tests/test_agent_layout.py -q

Expected:

  • documentation assertions fail until the docs are updated

Step 3: Write the minimal implementation

Update the docs so they describe:

  • one long-running service
  • CLI-managed periodic sync
  • sync-once as the bounded update command

Keep the operational guidance concise and cross-platform.

Step 4: Run tests to verify they pass

Run:

pytest tests/test_agent_layout.py -q

Expected:

  • documentation and layout assertions pass

Step 5: Commit

git add README.md AGENTS.md tests/test_agent_layout.py
git commit -m "docs: describe unified CLI-managed sync model"

items.title: Task 7: Run focused verification before completion
items.text:
Files: - Modify: none expected - Test: tests/test_services.py - Test: tests/test_sync.py - Test: tests/test_workspace.py - Test: tests/test_cli.py - Test: tests/test_agent_layout.py

Step 1: Run the focused test suite

Run:

pytest tests/test_services.py tests/test_sync.py tests/test_workspace.py tests/test_cli.py tests/test_agent_layout.py -q

Expected:

  • all targeted tests pass

Step 2: Run a quick CLI smoke check

Run:

python -m marrow_core.cli --help
python -m marrow_core.cli sync-once --help

Expected:

  • both commands print help successfully

Step 3: Review the rendered service output

Run:

python -m marrow_core.cli install-service --config marrow.toml --platform linux --output-dir /tmp/marrow-service-check
python -m marrow_core.cli install-service --config marrow.toml --platform darwin --output-dir /tmp/marrow-service-check-darwin

Expected:

  • only the primary service file is emitted for each platform

Step 4: Commit any final doc or test-only adjustments

git add .
git commit -m "test: verify unified sync runtime behavior"

items.title: Goal
items.text:
Decouple all non-core bot behavior from marrow-core into a new marrow-bot repository so that:

  • marrow-core remains a minimal, reusable scheduler + runtime library.
  • marrow-bot becomes the canonical default bot profile (roles, prompts, context, config).

Target outcome matches Issue #32: move non-core mechanisms to marrow-bot while keeping the core service model and contracts stable.

Related: marrow 分层架构与 OKR/产出设计 — four-layer model (core / infra-plugin / application / evolution), OKR–Thread–Task relationship, outcome types per responsibility, and core extension for custom background services (e.g. embedded web / dashboard).

items.title: What stays in marrow-core
items.text:
• Python packages
  • marrow_core/contracts.py
    • Defines workspace topology and configuration contracts.
    • Expresses how to locate roles, prompts, context, and runtime directories.
    • No hard-coded knowledge of specific role names or bot behaviors.
  • marrow_core/prompting.py
    • Assembles prompts from configured roles/, prompts/, and context.d/.
    • Treats these as external inputs, not as repo-owned content.
  • marrow_core/runtime.py
    • Socket, queue, and binary path resolution.
  • marrow_core/task_queue.py
    • Filesystem-backed queue operations.
  • marrow_core/services.py
    • launchd/systemd service rendering (marrow-heart.service, com.marrow.heart.plist).
  • marrow_core/scaffold.py
    • Workspace scaffold and starter config generation, parameterized by paths.
  • marrow_core/heartbeat.py
    • Scheduled orchestration per configured L1 main.
  • marrow_core/ipc.py
    • Local Unix-socket control plane.
  • marrow_core/cli.py
    • User-facing CLI surface (run, run-once, sync-once, setup, scaffold, validate, doctor, status, install-service, task *).

  • Any small helpers (config.py, sync.py, etc.) that are required to keep the above contract coherent.

  • Docs

  • AGENTS.md
    • Describes contracts: directory layout, IPC surface, CLI entrypoints, expected result codes.
    • Describes generic runtime layout under /opt/marrow-core and /Users/marrow.
    • Does not define concrete bot roles, behaviors, or handoff semantics.

  • README.md

    • Explains installation and upgrade of marrow-core as a library + service.
    • Documents CLI commands and configuration fields at a generic level.
    • Refers to external bot profiles (e.g. marrow-bot) for concrete roles and workflows.

  • Shell / service artifacts

  • setup.sh
    • Installs/updates the venv and marrow-core package.
    • Renders and installs the single heartbeat service for the platform.
    • Does not assume or manipulate a particular bot profile.
  • lib.sh, marrow-heart.service, com.marrow.heart.plist
    • Shared helper logic and templates required to run the heartbeat loop and sync model.

items.title: What moves to marrow-bot
items.text:
• Prompt & role content
  • roles/ tree (all l1/, l2/, l3/ roles).
  • roles.toml model-tier and casting configuration.
  • prompts/rules.md and any additional bot-specific rules.

  • Any future prompt/policy files that define concrete bot behavior or tone.

  • Context providers

  • context.d/*.py that express bot-specific monitoring, task generation, or workspace exploration.

  • Any additional context providers that are not strictly required for the core runtime to function.

  • Bot-specific configuration

  • Default marrow.toml that configures agents such as scout, conductor, and refit.

  • Recommended workspace paths and agent commands for the default bot profile.

  • Bot-facing docs and workflows

  • Detailed AGENTS.md for bot behavior:
    • Role responsibilities and delegation policy.
    • Handoff directory semantics (runtime/handoff/*).
    • Example workflows for human/agent collaboration.

  • A README.md in marrow-bot that explains:

    • How to install marrow-core.
    • How to install and enable the marrow-bot profile.
    • How to update both core and bot cleanly.

  • Bot installation helpers

  • Scripts such as setup-bot.sh that:
    • Validate that marrow-core is installed.
    • Cast roles/ into .opencode/agents/ via role-forge.
    • Copy or reference marrow-bot’s marrow.toml.
    • Optionally call marrow-core CLI (setup, install-service, sync-once) with the right config.

items.title: Design Principles
items.text:
• Core is profile-agnostic
  • marrow-core must not embed knowledge of specific roles (like scout, conductor, refit).
  • All such knowledge lives in marrow-bot (or any other bot profile).

  • Core APIs accept paths/configuration and operate on those, regardless of their contents.

  • Stable runtime contracts

  • Keep CLI surface, IPC protocol, directory layout, and exit codes stable across the split.

  • marrow-bot is responsible for staying within these contracts; marrow-core does not depend on bot internals.

  • Pluggable profiles

  • Treat marrow-bot as “just one profile” that consumes the marrow-core runtime.

  • Future profiles should be able to depend only on the documented contracts, not on private internals.

  • Minimal migration for existing users

  • Existing installations should be able to:
    • Upgrade marrow-core (runtime only).
    • Install marrow-bot to recover the current roles & behavior.
  • The service model (single heartbeat service + CLI-managed sync) must remain the same.

items.title: Migration Outline
items.text:
1. Abstract configuration and paths
  2. Audit marrow_core/contracts.py, runtime.py, scaffold.py, and related modules for assumptions that roles/, prompts/, or context.d/ live inside the marrow-core repo.

  3. Replace any hard-coded repo-relative paths with:

    • Configuration-driven paths from marrow.toml (core_dir, workspace, roles_dir, context_dirs, etc.).
    • Reasonable defaults that do not require repo-local prompt content.

  4. Move non-core content into marrow-bot

  5. Create a new marrow-bot repository.

  6. Copy the following from marrow-core into marrow-bot:

    • roles/
    • roles.toml
    • prompts/rules.md
    • context.d/*.py
    • The default marrow.toml (or equivalent), adjusted to reference marrow-bot locations as needed.

  7. Simplify core docs and setup

  8. Update README.md:
    • Focus on installing/updating marrow-core as a runtime.
    • Refer to marrow-bot for a default role/profile bundle.
  9. Update AGENTS.md:
    • Keep only architecture, filesystem layout, CLI/IPC contracts.
    • Remove role-specific behavior descriptions and move them to marrow-bot.

  10. Update setup.sh:

    • Remove any logic that assumes or manipulates repo-local roles/ or bot state.
    • Keep venv creation, package installation, and service installation.

  11. Introduce a clean bot installation path

  12. In marrow-bot, add:

    • A README.md documenting how to install marrow-core and then enable marrow-bot.
    • A helper script (setup-bot.sh or similar) that:
    • Ensures marrow-core is available.
    • Runs role-forge casting for roles/.
    • Copies or links marrow-bot’s marrow.toml into the workspace.
    • Invokes marrow-core CLI commands as needed to finish setup.

  13. Verify behavior parity

  14. Ensure that with both repos installed:
    • The effective runtime behavior (roles, handoffs, scheduling) matches the pre-split state.
  15. Ensure that with only marrow-core installed:
    • CLI and service installation still work.
    • Commands that require a profile fail clearly with actionable errors (“no roles/profile configured”).

items.title: Compatibility Considerations
items.text:
• Existing installations
  • Document a migration path:
    • Upgrade marrow-core in place.
    • Install marrow-bot and point marrow.toml to the new profile.

  • Consider adding a temporary compatibility note in README.md explaining the split and linking to marrow-bot.

  • Versioning

  • Bump marrow-core minor version to reflect the structural change.

  • Align marrow-bot’s initial version with the marrow-core version it expects.

  • Testing

  • Keep existing unit tests for marrow_core/* modules, adjusting imports and fixtures where they referred to repo-local roles/ or context.d/.
  • Add tests (in either repo) that:
    • Verify marrow-core behaves correctly with a minimal/no-profile config.
    • Verify marrow-bot integrates against the documented contracts.

items.title: marrow-bot Decoupling Implementation Plan
items.text:
For Claude: REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

Goal: Move all non-core bot behavior (roles, prompts, context, default config, bot-specific docs) out of marrow-core into a new marrow-bot repository, leaving marrow-core as a profile-agnostic runtime and scheduler.

Architecture: marrow-core exposes stable CLI, IPC, and filesystem contracts and knows nothing about concrete roles or bot workflows. marrow-bot (and future profiles) consume these contracts by providing roles/, roles.toml, prompts/, context.d/, and a marrow.toml configuration, plus helper scripts to integrate with the core runtime.

Tech Stack: Python (marrow-core), role-forge / OpenCode casting, shell scripts for setup, systemd / launchd for services, standard filesystem-based queues and state.

items.title: Task 1: Audit core assumptions about repo-local roles/prompts/context
items.text:
Files: - Modify: marrow_core/contracts.py - Modify: marrow_core/runtime.py - Modify: marrow_core/scaffold.py - Modify: marrow_core/config.py (if present and relevant) - Modify: marrow_core/prompting.py - Test: tests/test_workspace.py - Test: tests/test_runtime.py

Step 1: Identify hard-coded paths and assumptions

Inspect the listed modules for: - Direct references to roles/, prompts/, or context.d/ under the repo root. - Assumptions that these directories always exist or must be shipped with marrow-core. - Implicit coupling between specific role names (e.g. scout, conductor, refit) and the runtime.

Step 2: Document current behavior

For each identified assumption, write a short inline note in the implementation plan (not code comments) describing: - What the assumption is. - How it should change to become profile-agnostic (e.g. use config-driven paths, allow absence of content).

Step 3: Add or update tests that express expectations

In tests/test_workspace.py and tests/test_runtime.py: - Ensure there are tests that can be adapted to cover: - Behavior when no roles/config/profile is present (clean error messages). - Behavior when roles/config/profile are provided via configuration paths.

Run:

cd marrow-core
uv run pytest tests/test_workspace.py tests/test_runtime.py -q

Expected: tests pass (or fail only on cases we plan to update in later tasks).

items.title: Task 2: Make core profile-agnostic in code paths
items.text:
Files: - Modify: marrow_core/contracts.py - Modify: marrow_core/runtime.py - Modify: marrow_core/scaffold.py - Modify: marrow_core/prompting.py - Modify: marrow_core/config.py (if used for path defaults) - Test: tests/test_scaffold.py - Test: tests/test_services.py

Step 1: Parameterize roles/prompts/context paths

In the listed modules: - Replace any use of repo-relative roles/, prompts/, context.d/ with: - Paths derived from marrow.toml configuration (e.g. core_dir, workspace, roles_dir, context_dirs). - Reasonable defaults that do not require the repo to contain these directories. - Ensure that: - The system continues to support the current filesystem layout under /opt/marrow-core and /Users/marrow. - But those locations are not tied to a specific content bundle.

Step 2: Remove direct role name dependencies from core

In contracts.py, runtime.py, heartbeat.py, and related modules: - Remove or generalize any code that directly depends on role names like scout, conductor, or refit. - If role names are needed for tests or examples, move them to the test fixtures or docstrings, not core logic.

Step 3: Update scaffold behavior

In marrow_core/scaffold.py: - Ensure that workspace scaffolding: - Does not assume that canonical roles/ are present in the same repo. - Focuses on creating core runtime directories (runtime/state, runtime/handoff/*, etc.). - Leaves role/profile setup to external profiles (e.g. marrow-bot).

Step 4: Adjust and run tests

Update tests/test_scaffold.py, tests/test_services.py, and any related tests to: - Reflect the new profile-agnostic behavior. - Avoid depending on repo-local prompt/role content.

Run:

cd marrow-core
uv run pytest tests/test_scaffold.py tests/test_services.py -q

Expected: tests pass after adjustments.

items.title: Task 3: Extract non-core content into a new marrow-bot repository
items.text:
Files (source in marrow-core): - Move: roles/ (entire tree) - Move: roles.toml - Move: prompts/rules.md - Move: context.d/*.py - Move: default marrow.toml (if present in the repo root and representing the current bot profile)

Files (target in new repo): - Create: marrow-bot/roles/ - Create: marrow-bot/roles.toml - Create: marrow-bot/prompts/rules.md - Create: marrow-bot/context.d/ - Create: marrow-bot/marrow.toml

Step 1: Create marrow-bot repository structure

In a new directory (outside marrow-core): - Initialize a new git repository marrow-bot. - Create a minimal pyproject.toml or equivalent to document dependencies on marrow-core and role-forge.

Step 2: Copy non-core content

From marrow-core into marrow-bot: - Copy roles/ into marrow-bot/roles/. - Copy roles.toml into marrow-bot/roles.toml. - Copy prompts/rules.md into marrow-bot/prompts/rules.md. - Copy context.d/*.py into marrow-bot/context.d/. - Copy the default marrow.toml into marrow-bot/marrow.toml, adjusting paths if needed.

Step 3: Verify imports and references

In marrow-bot: - Ensure any Python modules under context.d/ still import only public APIs from marrow-core. - Remove or fix any imports that referenced marrow-core internals that may change.

No tests to run yet unless we add them in a later task; just ensure the content is syntactically valid.

items.title: Task 4: Simplify marrow-core docs to be profile-agnostic
items.text:
Files: - Modify: README.md - Modify: AGENTS.md - Modify: docs/plans/2026-03-13-marrow-bot-decoupling-design.md (if any clarifications are needed)

Step 1: Update README.md

  • Remove or rewrite sections that:
  • Describe specific roles (scout, conductor, refit) as part of marrow-core itself.
  • Embed default marrow.toml examples tied to the old in-repo profile.
  • Add:
  • A short explanation that marrow-core is profile-agnostic.
  • A link or reference to marrow-bot as the default bot profile for practical use.
  • An updated Quick Start that:
    • Installs marrow-core.
    • Mentions installing a profile such as marrow-bot for actual roles and workflows.

Step 2: Update AGENTS.md

  • Keep:
  • Architecture overview.
  • Filesystem layout for /opt/marrow-core and /Users/marrow/.
  • CLI surface and IPC behavior.
  • Remove or move:
  • Concrete bot role descriptions and handoff semantics to marrow-bot documentation.

Step 3: Sanity check doc rendering

Quickly view the updated markdown files to confirm they render correctly (no broken code fences or headings).

No tests to run; this is documentation-only but tightly coupled to user understanding.

items.title: Task 5: Adjust setup.sh and related shell helpers
items.text:
Files: - Modify: setup.sh - Modify: lib.sh (if it contains profile-specific logic) - Modify: any shell scripts under the repo root that reference roles/ or context.d/ - Test: run setup script in a controlled environment (e.g. local VM or container)

Step 1: Remove profile-specific steps from setup.sh

In setup.sh: - Remove logic that: - Assumes roles/ live inside the marrow-core repo. - Casts canonical roles into .opencode/agents/. - Manipulates bot-specific marrow.toml or profile configuration. - Keep logic that: - Creates/updates /opt/marrow-core/.venv. - Installs/updates the marrow-core Python package in that venv. - Renders and installs the single heartbeat service via marrow install-service.

Step 2: Confirm cross-platform behavior

Ensure that: - macOS path (com.marrow.heart.plist) is still rendered and installed correctly. - Linux path (marrow-heart.service) is still rendered and installed correctly.

Step 3: Dry-run setup

Run (without sudo if possible in a dev environment, or with a safe prefix):

cd /opt/marrow-core  # or the local checkout path
./setup.sh --help || true

If a dry-run or help mode is available, use it to verify that: - The script no longer refers to roles/ or context.d/. - The script focuses only on core installation tasks.

items.title: Task 6: Add marrow-bot installation helpers and docs
items.text:
Files (in marrow-bot repo): - Create: README.md - Create: AGENTS.md (bot-focused) - Create: setup-bot.sh

Step 1: Write marrow-bot README

In marrow-bot/README.md, document: - Prerequisite: marrow-core installation. - How to: - Clone marrow-bot. - Run setup-bot.sh (or equivalent) to: - Cast roles via role-forge. - Place marrow.toml in the right location. - Optionally invoke marrow-core CLI to validate and install services.

Step 2: Write AGENTS.md for bot

In marrow-bot/AGENTS.md: - Describe: - Each role’s responsibility (scout, conductor, refit, etc.). - Delegation rules and handoff semantics. - Example flows between agents and human operators.

Step 3: Implement setup-bot.sh

Create a shell script that: - Verifies marrow-core is installed (e.g. checks for marrow CLI or core directory). - Runs role-forge (or agent-caster) to cast roles/ into .opencode/agents/. - Places or symlinks marrow-bot/marrow.toml to the intended config path (e.g. /opt/marrow-core/marrow.toml or a user-specified location). - Optionally invokes:

marrow validate --config /path/to/marrow.toml
marrow install-service --config /path/to/marrow.toml --platform auto --output-dir ./service-out

Ensure the script is idempotent and safe to re-run.

items.title: Task 7: Verify end-to-end behavior and compatibility
items.text:
Files: - Existing tests under tests/ in marrow-core - Any new tests added for profile-agnostic behavior

Step 1: Run full test suite in marrow-core

From the marrow-core repo:

cd marrow-core
uv run pytest -q

Expected: - All tests pass. - No tests depend on repo-local roles/ or context.d/ existing as part of marrow-core.

Step 2: Manual smoke test with marrow-bot

In a dev environment: - Install updated marrow-core. - Install marrow-bot and run its setup script. - Start the service (via systemd/launchd or marrow run). - Verify: - Agents run as expected (e.g. scout heartbeats). - Tasks can be enqueued and processed via marrow task add/list. - Sync and status commands (marrow sync-once, marrow status) behave as before.

Step 3: Document migration notes

Add a short “Migration” section either to: - marrow-core/README.md, or - A dedicated docs/migration-marrow-bot-split.md (optional),

explaining: - How to upgrade from the old single-repo layout to marrow-core + marrow-bot. - What users need to change in their scripts/configs.

items.title: Task 8: Versioning and release coordination
items.text:
Files: - Modify: pyproject.toml (or equivalent) in marrow-core - Modify: pyproject.toml (or equivalent) in marrow-bot

Step 1: Bump marrow-core version

  • Increment the minor version of marrow-core to reflect the structural split.
  • Update any version references in documentation.

Step 2: Set initial marrow-bot version

  • Choose an initial marrow-bot version aligned with the marrow-core version it expects.
  • Document the compatible marrow-core version range in marrow-bot’s README.

Step 3: Tag and release

  • Tag a marrow-core release containing the decoupling changes.
  • Tag a matching marrow-bot release that works with that core version.

items.title: marrow 分层架构与 OKR/产出设计
path: marrow 分层架构与 OKR/产出设计

items.title: 目标
items.text:
在现有 marrow-bot 解耦设计 基础上，明确：

  1. 四层架构：核心层、基建/插件层、应用层、进化层；用户视角与 agent 职责边界。
  2. Core 扩展：支持自定义后台服务（含嵌入 Web），为后续 Dashboard 等插件提供基建；权限归属「谁嵌入谁拥有」。
  3. OKR ↔ Thread ↔ Task 关系，以及按职责的明确产出类型（PR、更新、试验结果、调研报告等；失败/证明不可行也算有效结果）。

本文档与解耦设计互补：解耦设计规定 core/bot 内容边界，本文规定层次模型、任务/产出语义与 core 的插件能力。
path: marrow 分层架构与 OKR/产出设计

items.title: 四层架构
path: marrow 分层架构与 OKR/产出设计

items.title: 层次定义
items.text: 层次 名称 职责 用户视角 典型归属 核心层 Core 最基础能力：调度、队列、同步、服务安装、IPC、配置契约、prompt 组装与 context 执行 用户可见且依赖 marrow-core 基建/插件层 Infra / Plugin 可选的扩展能力：自定义后台服务、嵌入 Web（如 Dashboard）、插件注册与生命周期 用户可视为「也是核心」的一部分 marrow-core 提供机制；Dashboard 等以插件形式接入 应用层 Application 具体 bot 行为：角色、委托策略、任务管线、交互与自我工作流程 用户主要关心 marrow-bot（及未来其他 profile） 进化层 Evolution 自进化：造轮子、反思、指标收集、MetaTask、产出可优化清单 Agent 自主负责；用户不直接配置 marrow-bot 内 MetaTask + 进化流程
path: marrow 分层架构与 OKR/产出设计

items.title: 用户与 Agent 的视角
items.text:
• 用户：主要接触「核心层 + 基建/插件层」（可统称为「平台」）和「应用层」；不直接设计进化层策略。
  • Agent：在应用层执行用户任务与自我工作；在进化层自主执行反思、造轮子立项与实现、产出量化与报告。

高内聚原则：核心层不依赖应用层或进化层；应用层依赖核心层（及可选基建）；进化层是应用层内的一组专门流程与角色（如 evolver、scout），不反向依赖核心实现细节。
path: marrow 分层架构与 OKR/产出设计

items.title: 核心层扩展：自定义后台服务与嵌入 Web
path: marrow 分层架构与 OKR/产出设计

items.title: 目标
items.text:
• marrow-core 提供机制：允许「在 heartbeat 或独立进程中」注册并运行自定义后台服务。
  • 典型用途：嵌入一个 Web 服务（谁嵌入，权限与数据归属即归谁），后续可在此之上搭建 Dashboard，作为插件机制的核心内容。
path: marrow 分层架构与 OKR/产出设计

items.title: 设计要点
items.text:
1. 契约
  2. 在 marrow.toml（或等价配置）中声明可选的后台服务列表，例如：
    • 类型：web / worker / 其他预留。
    • 入口：命令行或模块路径（由 profile/插件提供）。
    • 绑定地址/端口、环境变量等由配置或约定决定。

  3. Core 只负责：根据配置启动/停止子进程、可选地做健康检查与重启策略；不关心服务内部业务逻辑。

  4. 权限与归属

  5. 「谁嵌入谁拥有」：若 marrow-bot（或某 profile）声明并嵌入一个 Web 服务，则该服务的访问控制与数据范围由该 profile 的部署者决定；core 不强制统一鉴权模型，只保证服务以配置的用户/环境运行。

  6. 与现有服务模型的关系

  7. 现有 heartbeat 仍为单一主调度服务（launchd/systemd 等）。
  8. 自定义后台服务可以是：
    • 与 heartbeat 同进程内多协程/多线程（若实现简单），或
    • 独立子进程由 core 的「服务管理器」拉起（推荐，隔离性好）。

  9. 实现时需避免与当前 render_service_files 冲突：可先支持「由 heartbeat 或单独 systemd/launchd 单元」拉起一个「插件宿主进程」，再由该宿主按配置启动 Web 等子服务。

  10. Dashboard 作为插件

  11. Dashboard 作为「基建/插件层」的典型应用：以可选插件形式提供，依赖 core 的「自定义后台服务」能力。
  12. 数据来源：通过 core 已暴露的 IPC/队列/状态接口或约定目录下的文件；不要求 core 实现 HTTP API，Dashboard 可读本地文件或 Unix socket。

具体实现可放在后续实现计划中（如扩展 marrow_core/services.py 与配置 schema）。
path: marrow 分层架构与 OKR/产出设计

items.title: OKR、Thread、Task 关系与流程
path: marrow 分层架构与 OKR/产出设计

items.title: 概念层级
items.text:
• OKR（Objective / Key Result）
  • 顶层目标与关键结果，时间跨度较大（如一周、一月）。

  • 由应用层定义与维护；core 不解析 OKR 语义，只提供存储或透传（若需要）。

  • Thread

  • 一个连续工作流或主题线，对应一个 OKR 下的一个方向或一个「可独立执行的故事」。

  • 同一 Thread 内任务共享上下文（同一仓库、同一需求、同一实验）。

  • Task

  • 最小执行单元：有明确输入、执行步骤、必达产出类型（见下）。
  • 流程：任务要求 → 合并整理 → 加入/创建 Thread → 执行 → 产出记录。
path: marrow 分层架构与 OKR/产出设计

items.title: 流程（应用层，marrow-bot）
items.text:
1. 任务要求
  2. 外部输入（用户、Gateway、或 MetaTask）产生原始「任务要求」。
  3. 合并整理
  4. 规范化描述，映射到 OKR/Thread（若已有则挂到现有 Thread，否则创建 Thread，必要时关联或创建 OKR）。
  5. 加入/创建线程
  6. 确定或创建 thread_id，将任务作为 Task 入队，状态与优先级由应用层策略决定。
  7. 执行
  8. 由 L1/L2 角色按委托执行；core 提供队列与状态原语。
  9. 产出
  10. 每个 Task 必须落在一个明确产出类型上，并写入 Outcome（见下一节）。

OKR 与 Thread 的划分（供实现时细化）

  • 一个 OKR 对应一个周期内的顶层目标（如「本迭代交付 X」「建立 Y 能力」），可对应多个 Thread。
  • Thread 是 OKR 下的可执行工作流：同一需求、同一实验、同一仓库的连续任务归入同一 Thread；当主题切换或新需求/新实验开始时，可新建 Thread 并可选地挂到现有或新 OKR。
  • Task 永远属于某个 Thread；新建 Task 时若不存在合适 Thread，则先「合并整理」再创建 Thread 再入队。
  • Core 只关心 Task/Thread 的存储与调度；OKR 的创建、归档与展示可由应用层或 Dashboard 插件在 bot 侧维护。
path: marrow 分层架构与 OKR/产出设计

items.title: 按职责的产出类型（Outcome）
items.text:
不同职责对应不同的必须产出。以下类型为应用层约定，core 仅提供通用的 Outcome 存储（如 task_id, outcome_type, success, payload, metrics），由 marrow-bot 赋予语义。

职责/角色类型 产出类型 说明 失败/否定结果 交付/仓库跟进（如 courier, git-ops） PR 代码或配置变更以 PR 形式落地 明确说明「未开 PR」原因 + 替代产出（如 issue、文档链接） 对外发布/广播（如 herald） 更新 发布说明、changelog、公告等可追溯的更新记录 说明未发布原因及后续计划 验证/实验（如 verifier） 试验结果 可复现的通过/失败、日志、指标 失败或证明不可行也算有效结果，需记录结论与复现路径 探索/调研（如 scout, researcher） 调研/研究报告 结构化结论、选项对比、建议 结论可为「暂无可行方案」并附依据 归档与记忆（如 archivist） 归档记录 索引、链接、摘要 记录「未归档」原因 自进化（如 evolver） 变更集 + 指标 实际改动（prompt/role/工作流/轮子）+ 效果指标或反思摘要 记录「未采纳」原因与替代方案
path: marrow 分层架构与 OKR/产出设计

items.title: 产出记录的通用字段（建议）
items.text:
• task_id, thread_id, outcome_type, success（布尔或三值：success / failure_with_result / blocked）
  • artifact_ref：PR URL、文件路径、报告路径等
  • conclusion：短文本结论（尤其对「失败但算结果」的情况）
  • metrics：可选键值对（耗时、次数等）

Core 只需提供「按 task 写入/读取 Outcome」的接口与存储格式（如 JSON/TOML 文件或 SQLite 表），应用层负责按上表约束填写 outcome_type 与必填字段。
path: marrow 分层架构与 OKR/产出设计

items.title: 与现有解耦设计的关系
items.text:
• 核心层 / 基建层 对应解耦设计中的「marrow-core 保留内容」；本文新增 core 的「自定义后台服务 + 嵌入 Web」能力，不改变「core 不包含具体角色与 bot 行为」的边界。
  • 应用层 对应 marrow-bot：角色、prompt、context、任务管线与产出语义均在 marrow-bot。
  • 进化层 是应用层内由 evolver/scout 等驱动的 MetaTask 与造轮子流程，产出类型中已包含「自进化」一行。

实现时建议顺序：

  1. 在 core 中落成任务/线程/Outcome 的存储契约与最小 API（若尚未完全具备）。
  2. 在 marrow-bot 中落成 OKR/Thread/Task 与按职责的产出类型规范，并写入 AGENTS.md。
  3. 再在 core 中增加自定义后台服务与嵌入 Web 的配置与启动机制，最后接 Dashboard 插件。
path: marrow 分层架构与 OKR/产出设计

items.title: 文档与后续
items.text:
• 本设计保存于 docs/plans/2026-03-15-marrow-layers-okr-outcomes-design.md。
  • 解耦实施仍以 2026-03-13-marrow-bot-decoupling-design.md 及对应 implementation 计划为准。
  • OKR/Thread/Task 的详细数据结构和 marrow-bot 内 pipeline 的步骤，可在 marrow-bot 的 AGENTS.md 或单独 doc 中展开；本文确定层次与产出类型即可指导实现。
path: marrow 分层架构与 OKR/产出设计
